name: Claude 3
brainrotTitle: the one that actually reads the instructions
brainrotOneliner: constitutional AI, 200k context, and it will refuse to help you do crimes with more eloquence than any other model.

brainrotStats.totalParams: classified (Anthropic also won't tell us, respectfully)

keyInnovations: Constitutional AI — model is trained to critique its own outputs against a set of principles
200k token context window — one of the longest context windows at launch
RLHF from AI feedback (RLAIF) reduces dependence on human labelers
Tiered model family: Haiku (fast/cheap), Sonnet (balanced), Opus (capable)
brainrotSummary: Claude 3 is what happens when your entire company thesis is that safety and capability aren't at odds. Constitutional AI is genuinely innovative — the model learns to evaluate its own outputs. the 200k context window was a landmark. the refusals are eloquent. the vibes are different from OpenAI. this is the model that made people realize the competition was real.

comparisons.gpt2: GPT-2 is the origin. Claude 3 is the safety-first, constitutional-AI future GPT-2 couldn't have imagined.
comparisons.gpt3: Claude 3 is what you get when you spend years on alignment instead of just adding parameters.
comparisons.llama2: LLaMA 2 is open. Claude 3 is closed and constitutional. different philosophies on what responsible AI looks like.
comparisons.gpt4: the eternal matchup. benchmarks are close. vibes are different. both refusing to tell you their architecture.
comparisons.llama3-instruct: Claude 3 is proprietary and safety-focused. LLaMA 3 Instruct is open and getting better fast.
comparisons.deepseek-v3: Claude 3 is Anthropic's safety-first bet. DeepSeek V3 is China's efficiency-first bet. different worlds.
comparisons.mixtral-8x7b: Claude 3 is dense and aligned. Mixtral is sparse and open. completely different approaches to the same goal.
comparisons.grok-1: Claude 3 has a constitution. Grok-1 has chaos. different energy.
